revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 西根神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 福島県福島市飯坂町湯野字高畑2 |緯度度 = 37 | 緯度分 = 19 | 緯度秒 = 50.8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 27 | 経度秒 = 36.5 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = 佐藤新右衛門・古川善兵衛 |社格 = |創建 = [[1887年]]（[[明治]]20年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月14日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Fukushima }} '''西根神社'''（にしねじんじゃ）は[[福島県]][[福島市]][[飯坂町湯野]]高畑に鎮座する[[神社]]。
[[西根堰]]の開削に尽力した佐藤新右衛門と古川善兵衛の二人を祀っている。付近一帯を潤し、農業発展の礎となった西根堰の用水に感謝した農民が二人の徳を称えるため、西根郷33ヶ村4,231名の発願で、明治20年に建立された。秋季祭礼は、9月14日に行われる。境内は[[1792年]]（[[寛政]]4年）から[[明治時代]]にかけて[[刈谷藩]]の[[陣屋]]であった土地である。西側には石碑が立ち並んでおり、いずれも古川善兵衛の功をたたえるものである。西根堰上堰取水口付近の穴原地区にあったものや、[[飯坂温泉]]街にある稲荷屋旅館の屋敷内に建てられていたものも移設されている。
境内には'''高畑天満宮'''（たかはたてんまんぐう）も祀られており、高畑の天神様と呼ばれ地元で親しまれている。[[1月13日]]～16日、[[4月27日]]に「[[鷽替え|うそかえ祭]]」が行われ、延べ数万人が期間中に訪れ、無病息災や学業成就にご利益があるとされる木彫の鷽を買い求める。
{{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:にしねしんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島市の建築物]] [[Category:福島市の歴史]]

境内には'''高畑天満宮'''（たかはたてんまんぐう）も祀られており、高畑の天神様と呼ばれ地元で親しまれている。1月13日～16日、4月27日に「[[鷽替え|うそかえ祭]]」が行われ、延べ数万人が期間中に訪れ、無病息災や学業成就にご利益があるとされる木彫の鷽を買い求める。

revision.text:
'''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[Fukushima City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰}} during the early [[Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[1887]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the {{ill|Kariya Domain|ja|刈谷藩}}'s encampment from [[1792]] ({{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館}} in the {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden {{ill|Uso|ja|ウソ}} figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building completion ceremonies * Purification for renovations * Demolition purification
== Access == * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Iizaka Onsen Station|ja|飯坂温泉駅}} ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ Nishine Shrine - Official Iizaka Onsen Site] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html Fukushima Winter Festivals - Fukushima Machizukuri Center]

'''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[Fukushima City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰}} during the early [[Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[1887]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the {{ill|Kariya Domain|ja|刈谷藩}}'s encampment from [[1792]] ({{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館}} in the {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden {{ill|Uso|ja|ウソ}} figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building completion ceremonies * Purification for renovations * Demolition purification
== Access == * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Iizaka Onsen Station|ja|飯坂温泉駅}} ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ Nishine Shrine - Official Iizaka Onsen Site] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html Fukushima Winter Festivals - Fukushima Machizukuri Center]

The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. revision.text:
'''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[Fukushima City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰}} during the early [[Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[1887]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the {{ill|Kariya Domain|ja|刈谷藩}}'s encampment from [[1792]] ({{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館}} in the {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Uso-kae Festival}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden {{ill|Uso|ja|ウソ}} figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building completion ceremonies * Purification for renovations * Demolition purification
== Access == * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Iizaka Onsen Station|ja|飯坂温泉駅}} ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ Nishine Shrine - Official Iizaka Onsen Site] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html Fukushima Winter Festivals - Fukushima Machizukuri Center]

'''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[Fukushima City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰}} during the early [[Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[1887]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the {{ill|Kariya Domain|ja|刈谷藩}}'s encampment from [[1792]] ({{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館}} in the {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Uso-kae Festival}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden {{ill|Uso|ja|ウソ}} figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.

'''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[Fukushima City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰}} during the early [[Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[1887]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the {{ill|Kariya Domain|ja|刈谷藩}}'s encampment from [[1792]] ({{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館}} in the {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Uso-kae Festival}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. '''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[Fukushima City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰}} during the early [[Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[1887]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the {{ill|Kariya Domain|ja|刈谷藩}}'s encampment from [[1792]] ({{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館}} in the {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉}} district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Uso-kae Festival}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden {{ill|Uso|ja|ウソ}} figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building completion tier 2]][[Category: ceremonies * Purification for renovations * Demolition purification
== Access == * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Iizaka Onsen Station|ja|飯坂温泉駅}} ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ Nishine Shrine - Official Iizaka Onsen Site] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html Fukushima Winter Festivals - Fukushima Machizukuri Center]

'''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[Fukushima City]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門|12=draft}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛|12=draft}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|12=draft}} during the early [[Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[1887]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the {{ill|Kariya Domain|ja|刈谷藩|12=draft}}'s encampment from [[1792]] ({{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|12=draft}} 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|ja|飯坂温泉|12=draft}} district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Uso-kae Festival|12=draft}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden {{ill|Uso|ja|ウソ|12=draft}} figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building completion tier 2]][[Category: ceremonies * Purification for renovations * Demolition purification
== Access == * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Iizaka Onsen Station|ja|飯坂温泉駅|12=draft}} ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ Nishine Shrine - Official Iizaka Onsen Site] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html Fukushima Winter Festivals - Fukushima Machizukuri Center]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 西根神社 |画像=[[File:Nishine-jinja,_Fukushima-city_10.jpg|240px]] |所在地 = [[福島県]][[福島市]]飯坂町湯野字高畑2 | 緯度度 = 37 | 緯度分 = 49 | 緯度秒 = 51.8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 27 | 経度秒 = 37.5 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-07 |祭神 = 佐藤新右衛門
古川善兵衛 |社格 = |創建 = [[1887年]]（[[明治]]20年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月14日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Fukushima }} '''西根神社'''（にしねじんじゃ）は[[福島県]][[福島市]][[飯坂町湯野]]高畑に鎮座する[[神社]]。 == 特徴 == [[江戸時代]]初期に[[西根堰]]の開削に尽力した[[西根堰#佐藤新右衛門|佐藤新右衛門]]と[[西根堰#古河善兵衛|古川善兵衛]]の二人を祀っている。付近一帯を潤し、農業発展の礎となった西根堰の用水に感謝した農民が二人の徳を称えるため、西根郷33ヶ村4,231名の発願で、明治20年に建立された。秋季祭礼は、9月14日に行われる。境内は[[1792年]]（[[寛政]]4年）から[[明治時代]]にかけて[[刈谷藩]]の[[陣屋]]であった土地である。西側には石碑が立ち並んでおり、いずれも古川善兵衛の功をたたえるものである。西根堰上堰取水口付近の穴原地区にあったものや、[[飯坂温泉]]街にある稲荷屋旅館の屋敷内に建てられていたものも移設されている。 境内には'''高畑天満宮'''（たかはたてんまんぐう）も祀られており、高畑の[[天神信仰|天神様]]と呼ばれ地元で親しまれている。1月13日～16日、4月27日に「[[鷽替え|うそかえ祭]]」が行われ、延べ数万人が期間中に訪れ、無病息災や学業成就にご利益があるとされる木彫の[[ウソ|鷽]]を買い求める。 == 年中行事 == {| class="wikitable" |+ !日付 ! |- |1月1日～3日 |初詣 |- |1月中頃 |西根神社「どんと祭」 高畑天満宮「うそかえ祭」 |- |5月3日 |春季大祭 |- |9月14日 |秋季大祭 |- |12月23日 |歳の市 |} == 御祈祷 == === 殿内祈祷 === * 家内安全 * 商売繁盛 * お車のお祓い * 安産祈願 * 初宮まいり * 厄祓い * 方位除け * 七五三まいり * 合格祈願 * 病気平癒 === 出張祭典 === * 地鎮祭 * 竣工際 * 増改築清祓い * 解体清祓い == 交通アクセス == *[[飯坂温泉駅]]より徒歩9分（{{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=経路案内}}）。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[西根堰]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ 西根神社 - 飯坂温泉オフィシャルサイト] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html 福島の冬祭り - 福島まちづくりセンター] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:にしねしんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島市の建築物]] [[Category:福島市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[西根神社]] to [[Nishine Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city]][[ja:西根神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city 13.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Fukushima|qid=Q161176}}|coordinates={{Coord|37.83105555555556|N|140.46041666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30937067]] Wikidata name: Nishine-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[:en:Fukushima City|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Shin'emon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Zenbei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|qq=|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}} during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[:en:1887|1887]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[:en:September 14|September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the [[:en:Kariya Domain|Kariya Domain]]'s encampment from [[:en:1792|1792]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariya Ryokan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Iizaka Onsen|Iizaka Onsen]] district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Bush Warbler Replacement|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-Kae Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-kae Festival|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676444}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden [[:en:Eurasian bullfinch|Uso]] figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building Completion tier 2]][[Category: ceremonies * Purification for renovations * Demolition purification
== Access == * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Iizaka Onsen Station|Iizaka Onsen Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|qq=|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ Nishine Shrine - Official Iizaka Onsen Site] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html Fukushima Winter Festivals - Fukushima Machizukuri Center]

Wooden [[:en:Eurasian bullfinch|Uso]] figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building Completion tier 2]][[Category: ceremonies * Purification for renovations * Demolition purification
== Access == * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Iizaka Onsen Station|Iizaka Onsen Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[d:" to "[[da:d:"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city]][[ja:西根神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city 13.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Fukushima|WD=Q161176}}|coordinates={{Coord|37.83105555555556|N|140.46041666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30937067]] Wikidata name: Nishine-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[:en:Fukushima City|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Shin'emon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Zenbei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|qq=|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}} during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[:en:1887|1887]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[:en:September 14|September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the [[:en:Kariya Domain|Kariya Domain]]'s encampment from [[:en:1792|1792]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariya Ryokan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Iizaka Onsen|Iizaka Onsen]] district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Bush Warbler Replacement|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-Kae Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-kae Festival|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676444}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden [[:en:Eurasian bullfinch|Uso]] figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building Completion tier 2]][[Category: ceremonies * Purification for renovations * Demolition purification
== Access == * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Iizaka Onsen Station|Iizaka Onsen Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|qq=|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ Nishine Shrine - Official Iizaka Onsen Site] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html Fukushima Winter Festivals - Fukushima Machizukuri Center]

Wooden [[:en:Eurasian bullfinch|Uso]] figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.

=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building Completion tier 2]][[Category: ceremonies * Purification for renovations * Demolition purification
== Access == * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Iizaka Onsen Station|Iizaka Onsen Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).

<!--Nishine Shrine--><!--Nishine Shrine--> [[Category:Buildings in Fukushima City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukushima, Hokkaidō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city}} {{wikidata link|Q30937067}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city]][[ja:西根神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city 13.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Fukushima|WD=Q161176}}|coordinates={{Coord|37.83105555555556|N|140.46041666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30937067]] Wikidata name: Nishine-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[:en:Fukushima City|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Shin'emon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Zenbei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}} during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[:en:1887|1887]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[:en:September 14|September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the [[:en:Kariya Domain|Kariya Domain]]'s encampment from [[:en:1792|1792]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariya Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Iizaka Onsen|Iizaka Onsen]] district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Bush Warbler Replacement|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-Kae Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-kae Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676444}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden [[:en:Eurasian bullfinch|Uso]] figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building Completion tier 2]][[Category: ceremonies * Purification for renovations * Demolition purification
== Access == * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Iizaka Onsen Station|Iizaka Onsen Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ Nishine Shrine - Official Iizaka Onsen Site] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html Fukushima Winter Festivals - Fukushima Machizukuri Center]

<!--Nishine Shrine--><!--Nishine Shrine--> [[Category:Buildings in Fukushima City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukushima, Hokkaidō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city}} {{wikidata link|Q30937067}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city]][[ja:西根神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city]][[ja:西根神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city 13.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Fukushima|WD=Q161176}}|coordinates={{Coord|37.83105555555556|N|140.46041666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30937067]] Wikidata name: Nishine-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[:en:Fukushima City|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Shin'emon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Zenbei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}} during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[:en:1887|1887]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[:en:September 14|September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the [[:en:Kariya Domain|Kariya Domain]]'s encampment from [[:en:1792|1792]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariya Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Iizaka Onsen|Iizaka Onsen]] district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Bush Warbler Replacement|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-Kae Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-kae Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676444}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden [[:en:Eurasian bullfinch|Uso]] figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building Completion tier 2]][[Category: ceremonies * Purification for renovations * Demolition purification
== Access == * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Iizaka Onsen Station|Iizaka Onsen Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ Nishine Shrine - Official Iizaka Onsen Site] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html Fukushima Winter Festivals - Fukushima Machizukuri Center]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{draft categories|↵↵}}" to ""
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city]][[ja:西根神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city]][[ja:西根神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city]][[ja:西根神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city 13.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Fukushima|WD=Q161176}}|coordinates={{Coord|37.83105555555556|N|140.46041666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30937067]] Wikidata name: Nishine-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[:en:Fukushima City|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Shin'emon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Zenbei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}} during the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. The shrine was established in [[:en:1887|1887]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on [[:en:September 14|September 14]]. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the [[:en:Kariya Domain|Kariya Domain]]'s encampment from [[:en:1792|1792]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariya Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Iizaka Onsen|Iizaka Onsen]] district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Bush Warbler Replacement|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-Kae Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-kae Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676444}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden [[:en:Eurasian bullfinch|Uso]] figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building Completion tier 2]] ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ Nishine Shrine - Official Iizaka Onsen Site] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html Fukushima Winter Festivals - Fukushima Machizukuri Center]

The autumn festival is held annually on {{ill|September 14|en|September 14|WD=Q2847|lt=September 14}}. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the {{ill|Kariya Domain|en|Kariya Domain|WD=Q1045776|lt=Kariya Domain}}'s encampment from {{ill|1792|en|1792|WD=Q7805|lt=1792}} ({{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariya Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|en|Iizaka Onsen|WD=Q17192714|lt=Iizaka Onsen}} district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Bush Warbler Replacement|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-Kae Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-kae Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676444}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden {{ill|Eurasian bullfinch|en|Eurasian bullfinch|WD=Q25382|lt=Uso}} figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building Completion tier 2]] ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).

The autumn festival is held annually on {{ill|September 14|en|September 14|WD=Q2847|lt=September 14}}. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the {{ill|Kariya Domain|en|Kariya Domain|WD=Q1045776|lt=Kariya Domain}}'s encampment from {{ill|1792|en|1792|WD=Q7805|lt=1792}} ({{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariya Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|en|Iizaka Onsen|WD=Q17192714|lt=Iizaka Onsen}} district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Bush Warbler Replacement|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-Kae Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-kae Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676444}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden {{ill|Eurasian bullfinch|en|Eurasian bullfinch|WD=Q25382|lt=Uso}} figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.

revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Completion tier 2]] (crud category cleanup)
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city]][[ja:西根神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city]][[ja:西根神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city]][[ja:西根神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Nishine-jinja, Fukushima-city 13.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Fukushima|WD=Q161176|1=Fukushima (city)}}|coordinates={{Coord|37.83105555555556|N|140.46041666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Nishine Shrine''' (西根神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Iizaka-machi Yunozawa]], [[:en:Fukushima City|Fukushima City]], {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}.
== Features == Nishine Shrine enshrines two individuals, {{ill|Sato Shin'emon|ja|佐藤新右衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Shin'emon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Furukawa Zenbei|ja|古川善兵衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furukawa Zenbei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who contributed significantly to the development of the {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}} during the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. The shrine was established in {{ill|1887|en|1887|WD=Q7826|lt=1887|1=1887}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 20) by 4,231 farmers from 33 villages in Nishine to honor their contributions, which greatly benefited agriculture in the area. The autumn festival is held annually on {{ill|September 14|en|September 14|WD=Q2847|lt=September 14|1=September 14}}. The shrine grounds were previously the site of the {{ill|Kariya Domain|en|Kariya Domain|WD=Q1045776|lt=Kariya Domain|1=Kariya Domain}}'s encampment from {{ill|1792|en|1792|WD=Q7805|lt=1792|1=1792}} ({{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei|1=Kansei}} 4) to the Meiji period. Stone monuments on the western side of the shrine commemorate Furukawa Zenbei's accomplishments, with some relocated from the vicinity of the Nishine Canal’s upper intake and other areas like the {{ill|Inariya Ryokan|ja|稲荷屋旅館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariya Ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|en|Iizaka Onsen|WD=Q17192714|lt=Iizaka Onsen|1=Iizaka Onsen}} district.
The grounds also house the '''Takahata Tenmangu''' (高畑天満宮), a shrine devoted to Tenjin worship, popularly known as the "Takahata Tenjin-sama." The shrine holds the {{ill|Uso-Kae Festival|ja|鷽替え|1=Bush Warbler Replacement|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-Kae Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Uso-kae Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676444}} from January 13 to 16 and on April 27, attracting tens of thousands of visitors seeking blessings for good health and academic success. Wooden {{ill|Eurasian bullfinch|en|Eurasian bullfinch|WD=Q25382|lt=Uso|1=Eurasian bullfinch}} figurines are sold during the festival for their protective charms.
== Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Date !Event |- |January 1–3 |New Year's Visit (Hatsumode) |- |Mid-January |Nishine Shrine "Dondo Festival" Takahata Tenmangu "Uso-Kae Festival" |- |May 3 |Spring Grand Festival |- |September 14 |Autumn Grand Festival |- |December 23 |Year-End Market |}
== Prayer Services ==
=== In-Shrine Prayers === * Household safety * Prosperous business * Vehicle purification * Safe childbirth * First shrine visit for newborns * Yakubarai (purification from misfortune) * Directional avoidance * Shichi-Go-San festival prayers * Academic success * Recovery from illness
=== Off-Site Rituals === * Groundbreaking ceremonies * Building Completion tier 2]] ({{OSM経路図|start=37.83045,140.45473|goal=37.83095,140.46043|name=Route Map}}).
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Nishine Irrigation Canal|ja|西根堰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishine Irrigation Canal|1=Nishine-zeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628784}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iizaka.com/join/join07/shiseki19/ Nishine Shrine - Official Iizaka Onsen Site] * [http://www.fmcnet.co.jp/omaturi22/nisineindex.html Fukushima Winter Festivals - Fukushima Machizukuri Center]
